title: Vicia subrotunda
intro: Vicia subrotunda, also known as the small bird's foot or narrow-leaved vetch, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Vicia subrotunda include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Vicia subrotunda is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification places Vicia subrotunda among the large and diverse group of plants known as legumes. The genus Vicia is further categorized within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. Vicia subrotunda is a member of the tribe Fabeae, a subgroup within the Faboideae subfamily. By classifying Vicia subrotunda within these groups, botanists can better understand the plant's relationships to other similar species.
uses: Vicia subrotunda, also known as the round vetch, has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its roots and leaves are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Additionally, Vicia subrotunda is said to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which may help in wound healing and preventing infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Vicia subrotunda.

The medicinal parts of Vicia subrotunda are primarily its leaves and flowers, which are rich in bioactive compounds.
